questions.scenario: A pull request adds a bucket resource with tags, environment, and name. What is the main benefit over creating it silently in the console?

options.text: The intended bucket shape can be reviewed before it exists.
The bucket can never be deleted by anyone.
The team no longer needs cloud knowledge.

questions.explanation: IaC files make infrastructure intent visible before the real system changes.

questions.scenario: An Ansible task says Nginx should be installed and running. Why is that still infrastructure code?

options.text: It describes desired server configuration in a versioned file.
Only cloud networks count as infrastructure.
YAML files automatically make all systems secure.
It avoids the need to connect to hosts.

questions.explanation: IaC can describe cloud resources or host configuration; the important part is reviewed desired infrastructure in files.

questions.scenario: Which qualities make IaC files useful as a team source of truth?

options.text: They can be diffed in review.
They can be searched and versioned.
They can express intended ownership and environment.
They make every provider setting obvious without reading docs.

questions.explanation: Files help with review, history, and ownership, but engineers still need provider understanding.
